minor_criteria.xerosis: Generalized dry skin, often persistent and non-seasonal.
minor_criteria.ichthyosis_palmar_keratosis: Presence of ichthyosis (fish-scale skin), palmar hyperlinearity (excessive palm lines), or keratosis pilaris (small bumps on outer arms/thighs).
minor_criteria.type_1_reactivity: Immediate skin-test reactivity to common environmental allergens.
minor_criteria.elevated_ige: Elevated serum IgE levels above laboratory reference range.
minor_criteria.early_age_onset: Symptom onset typically occurring before the age of 5.
minor_criteria.cutaneous_infections: Tendency toward skin infections, particularly Staphylococcus aureus or Herpes simplex (eczema herpeticum).
minor_criteria.hand_foot_dermatitis: Non-specific chronic dermatitis localized to the hands or feet.
minor_criteria.nipple_eczema: Eczematous lesions specifically involving the nipple or areola (often adolescent/adult onset).
minor_criteria.cheilitis: Inflammation, cracking, or scaling of the lips.
minor_criteria.recurrent_conjunctivitis: Frequent episodes of inflammation of the conjunctiva.
minor_criteria.dennie_morgan_fold: A characteristic double fold of skin under the lower eyelid (infraorbital).
minor_criteria.keratoconus: Conical protrusion of the cornea (often associated with eye rubbing).
minor_criteria.anterior_subcapsular_cataracts: Characteristic lens opacities located under the anterior capsule.
minor_criteria.orbital_darkening: Dark circles under the eyes, often referred to as 'allergic shiners'.
minor_criteria.facial_pallor_erythema: Episodes of flushing or abnormal paleness of the face.
minor_criteria.pityriasis_alba: Hypopigmented (light-colored), scaly patches, common on the face and upper arms in children.
minor_criteria.itch_when_sweating: Pruritus triggered or significantly worsened by perspiration.
minor_criteria.intolerance_wool_solvents: Immediate skin irritation or itching upon contact with wool clothing or lipid-dissolving solvents.
minor_criteria.perifollicular_accentuation: Prominent, small bumps around hair follicles, particularly common in darker skin types (Fitzpatrick IV-VI).
minor_criteria.food_intolerance: Clinical sensitivity or allergic reactions to specific foods.
minor_criteria.environmental_emotional_influence: Exacerbation of symptoms due to stress, heat, humidity, or seasonal changes.
minor_criteria.white_dermographism: Skin turns white instead of red when stroked with a firm object.
minor_criteria.delayed_blanch: Delayed whitening of the skin following the application of certain vasoactive agents.

major_criteria.pruritus: Severe itching, often the primary patient complaint.
major_criteria.distribution: Age-dependent: Flexural lichenification in adults; facial and extensor involvement in infants.
major_criteria.chronic_history: History of relapsing or persistent skin inflammation over months/years.
major_criteria.atopy_history: Personal or family history of asthma, allergic rhinitis, or atopic dermatitis.

grading_rule: Definitive diagnosis requires at least 3 Major criteria AND at least 3 Minor criteria.
